name: Focaccia Pugliese (Focaccia from Puglia)
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the potatoes and boil them until they are tender. Drain and mash them.
2. Stir the yeast into the warm water in a large mixing bowl.
3. Add the flour, mashed potatoes, and salt to the mixture in two additions.
4. Mix together thoroughly.
5. Knead for 10 minutes. Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and let rise until doubled.
6. Divide the dough into half and shape each into a ball.
7. Place each ball in an oiled 9-inch baking pan and stretch the dough towards the edges.
8. Cover, let sit for 10 minutes, then stretch a little more.
9. Cover again and let it double.
10. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
11. Dimple the dough with your finger.
12. Sprinkle with olive oil and spread with tomatoes, capers, salt, and oregano.
13. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes until golden.
14. Cool on wire racks and eat at room temperature.
